chunks.text: ) Remember to avoid drinking through a straw, no carbonated beverages, and no forceful spitting for three days. Cold food items will feel soothing to your childs mouth (ice cream, popsicles, etc. ). Please avoid hot items like soup because your child may burn themselves as well as the heat will promote bleeding. Room temperature and cold foods are recommended. (i. e. macaroni and cheese, mashed potatoes, etc.

Julie also is a Diplomate of the American Board of Pediatric Dentistry and serves as a consultant with the Board. Dr. Julie has served the community both internationally and locally. She has volunteered for mission trips to Mexico and Honduras. In the DFW metroplex, she has helped with Christinas Smile, Save-A-Smile, Mission Arlington, and Tarrant County Dental Hygiene program. Dr.

Firewheel Dentistry for Kids – Your Pediatric Dentist in Garland TX

--- IV Sedation ---
IV Sedation IV Sedation: We have a great team of anesthesiologists to place your child to sleep while all their dental treatment is completed at once. Request an Appointment

--- Staff ---
Meet Our Family Dr. Julie David Talaiver is a native of the Dallas area. She received her undergraduate degree in Biochemistry from Austin College in Sherman, TX.

Julie is a member of the American Dental Association, Texas Dental Association, American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry, College of Diplomates with the American Board of Pediatric Dentistry, and Greater Dallas Pediatric Dental Society. Tibisay Tibi is our newest pediatric dental assistant. She has many years in pediatric dentistry and excited to have her join our team. Tibi has two sons that keep her busy.

She attended Baylor College of Dentistry where she earned her Doctor of Dental Surgery degree in 2004. Dr. Julie earned her certificate in pediatric dentistry from the University of Southern California. She completed her hospital residency at Childrens Hospital of Orange County and the non-profit Healthy Smiles for Kids of Orange County. She gained tremendous training in the health of infants, children, adolescents, and the care of special needs children. Dr.

She has two cute little ones that keep her busy throughout the week. Tonya Tonya is a Registered Dental Assistant who has been in the dental field for many years with great experience. It is a treat when she is able to assist our patients. She has 1 daughter and 2 sons and enjoys spending time with her gorgeous family. We are excited to welcome her future grandson. --- Space Maintainers ---
Space Maintainers Remember to brush toward the gums to remove plaque on the appliance.

It is normal for oozing to continue for the next few days. It is also normal for oozing to occur after brushing or eating. Remember to bite down (firm pressure) on gauze to help control the bleeding. If you depleted your supply of cotton gauzes, you can place a tea bag on the extraction site and have your child bite down for 30 minutes. Please avoid strenuous activity for 24 hours. (No swimming, biking, running, sports practice, etc.

Alejandra Alejandra helps in the front desk to greet our patients and also helps assist us with xrays. Her future aspirations include being accepted into a dental hygiene program. We wish her best of luck. Michelle Michelle handles both front desk and also a chairside assistant. We are so proud of her for being accepted into dental hygiene school. Nicole Nicole is our super mom who helps us on Fridays to verify insurance. She is our insurance specialist.

Please avoid any sticky candy (Jolly Ranchers, caramel, etc. ). If your childs space maintainer is displaced, please save the space maintainer and call us for an appointment ASAP before tooth movement occurs. --- Extractions ---
Extractions Please read local anesthesia. Please give your child Childrens Ibuprofen or Tylenol as soon as possible so they may be comfortable as the numbing medicine wears away. Have your child continue to bite down on gauze for 30 minutes to help control bleeding.

If your child is not cooperative, Dr. Julie will discuss other modalities to complete your childs dental treatment safely. Request an Appointment

--- Sedation ---
Nitrous Oxide Sedation for Kids in Garland, TX Sedation Options: Nitrous Oxide (laughing gas) IV Sedation Request an Appointment

--- Your First Visit ---
Your First Visit The American Academy of Pediatric Dentists recommends a dental home to be established by your childs first birthday.

--- Post-Op ---
Post-Op Post-Operative Instructions: Local Anesthesia White Fillings Crowns Extractions Space Maintainers Request an Appointment

--- Oral Sedation ---
Oral Sedation Oral Sedation (Giggly juice): Oral sedation is medicine the child will drink and will be used in combination with nitrous oxide to further relax your child. Once the medication is administered, it will be about 45-60 minutes before the medicine will have taken effect.

There is no repair that can be done to fix a broken white crown. --- Composite Fillings ---
Composite (White colored) Fillings Please see local anesthesia. A clear sealant was placed above your childs white-colored filling. Initially, the filling will feel thick when your child bites down, but it will wear down to the normal bite within two days. If this feeling persists after one week, please call our office for a complimentary adjustment. Please avoid chewing on ice, hard candy, corn nuts, etc.

After treatment is completed, the child will breathe 100% oxygen which will push out all the nitrous oxide from the lungs. That way, your child is ready to go home quickly after treatment. A common side effect of laughing gas is nausea and vomiting. If your child is prone to motion sickness, please let Dr. Julie know. Also, the gas will not put your child to sleep. Nitrous Oxide is not a miracle gas that turns a completely uncooperative child into a child able to do the dental treatment.

Remember to brush towards the gums, especially where the new crown has been placed for the ideal healing of the gingival tissue. Please avoid any sticky candy (Jolly Ranchers, fruit snacks, Laffy Taffy, etc. ) or dried sticky fruit. If your childs crown is displaced, please save the crown and call us for an appointment to recement the crown. Remember to be careful with white crowns due to possible chipping of the white surface, and exposing the silver metal below.

The parent is allowed back in the treatment room with their child during the appointment. The success of completing dental treatment is highly dependent on the child. Your child may require multiple visits to complete their treatment needs. Your child may NOT go to school and will need to be supervised by an adult. Instructions BEFORE sedation: 1. Do not eat or drink anything after midnight of the night before your sedation appointment. Your child will arrive at our office with an empty stomach.

Your child will require supervision at home. Dr. Julie will tell you the instructions after treatment to take care of your child. Please feel free to call our office at 972-414-KIDS (5437) for any questions or concerns. Request an Appointment

--- Nitrous Oxide ---
Nitrous Oxide Nitrous Oxide (laughing gas): This is the most common sedative used in dentistry. Nitrous oxide is a gas that is inhaled that allows the child to be more relaxed and comfortable.

2. Please call if your child becomes sick or develops a fever, cough, sore throat, or has a runny nose. We will reschedule their appointment. 3. Dress your child in loose fitting, comfortable clothing. You may want to bring a light blanket or an extra change of clothing. 4. Your child must be accompanied by a parent or guardian who must stay in the waiting room or treatment room for the duration of the dental appointment. 5. Your child will be unable to go to school the day of the sedation.

for the life of the teeth to avoid fracturing the sealant or the tooth itself. --- Local Anesthesia ---
Local Anesthesia Your child will be numb for a minimum of one hour. Please watch your child to make sure they avoid chewing on their lip or cheeks. If your child chews on their cheek, it will leave a large ulcerated area on the cheek and will take about two weeks to heal. Be careful.

At this first appointment, we will have the opportunity to meet your child and provide invaluable information in building the foundation for your childs oral health. At Firewheel Dentistry for Kids, we have an exciting underwater theme where kids are thrilled to see different sea creatures in the office. Each child is able to choose and watch a movie while their dental treatment is being completed by our highly trained team.

It may help your child see what will happen on their first trip to the dentist. Request an Appointment

--- Policies ---
Policies OFFICE POLICY: Our patients are seen by appointment to reserve their time and also to be respectful of your time. We make every effort to see you at the appointed time. These dental appointments may vary according to a child’s needs and level of cooperation.

By staying on schedule, we can give every child the attention and care they deserve in as pleasant an atmosphere as possible. Parents are welcome to accompany their children in the treatment area or stay in our adult waiting room. We understand that everyones schedules are changing due to work schedules or children being sick. Please call our office to reschedule your childs appointment 24-48 hours in advance. This allows us to see children on the schedule sooner.

Operative appointments (fillings, extractions, crowns, etc. ) will ONLY be scheduled in the morning to allow ample procedural and recovery time. FINANCIAL POLICY: You are responsible for your childs dental fees at the time services are rendered by Dr. Julie Talaiver. We accept cash, check, VISA, Mastercard, Discover, and debit cards. There will be a charge for returned checks. Please read your insurance policy and be familiar with your benefits.

Julie will complete a thorough head and neck examination, including an examination of the teeth, gums, and how the teeth come together. Radiographs, if taken, will be evaluated and recommendations will be made for your childs teeth. We are fortunate to have an experienced network of orthodontists, oral surgeons, cosmetic dentists, endodontists, and periodontists to be able to provide comprehensive care for all our patients. Please visit our home page to see a video of our office.

Our office does not determine the dental benefits you have, rather your employer determines your benefits based upon the chosen plan. As a courtesy to you, we will be happy to complete and file insurance forms in regards to dental treatment completed. Please remember that payment for the first visit is expected at the time of the appointment. Payment for subsequent services will be your estimated share of cost at each visit as determined by your insurance company.

--- Sitemap ---

--- Thank You ---
Contact Form Received. Thank you for contacting us. Your request has been received.

The team member will introduce your child to all the equipment in the treatment area starting with the tooth camera all the way to our spin brush. Our kid-friendly language will put your child at ease as they become comfortable in their new environment. Please let us know if there are any special concerns regarding your child to ensure an appointment that will be tailored to your childs individual needs. As a board-certified pediatric dentist, Dr.

We appreciate your help in this matter. Our peak times are, of course, after school and holidays. These appointments are booked in advance. We suggest scheduling your 6 months cleaning appointment before leaving the office to ensure the day and time that would work best for your schedule. We are fortunate to be able to provide extended weekday hours and Saturday appointments. If you do not show for the appointment, you will not be able to reschedule the appointment during these peak times.

Our friendly staff will be in touch with you shortly, and we are all looking forward to welcoming you to our office. --- Services ---
Services Our Services Our office is proud to offer the following services to our patients: Sedation Local Anesthesia Composite Fillings Crowns Extractions Space Maintainers
